claim.summary: The plan does not define or enforce authentication/authorization for the new admin endpoint, and the proposed access model is a long-lived bearer token shared with an external Lambda. That leaves a PII-bearing endpoint exposed to credential leakage and does not establish least privilege, rotation, or revocation expectations.
claim.text: The plan does not define or enforce authentication/authorization for the new admin endpoint, and the proposed access model is a long-lived bearer token shared with an external Lambda. That leaves a PII-bearing endpoint exposed to credential leakage and does not establish least privilege, rotation, or revocation expectations.
claim.owner_summary: The plan does not define or enforce authentication/authorization for the new admin endpoint, and the proposed access model is a long-lived bearer token shared with an external Lambda. That leaves a PII-bearing endpoint exposed to credential leakage and does not establish least privilege, rotation, or revocation expectations.

claim.summary: The implementation violates the stated response contract by returning `user.to_dict()` instead of an explicit projection of `{id, email, tier, subscribed_at, last_active_at}`. That can leak unintended user fields and makes the API contract unstable for Marketing.

claim.summary: Cross-account PII egress (billing AWS → marketing AWS) lacks IAM-role-assumption / STS pattern. Bearer token = no audit trail at trust boundary, no per-call identity. Use IAM sigv4 + assumed role with `sts:ExternalId`, not static token.
claim.text: Cross-account PII egress (billing AWS → marketing AWS) lacks IAM-role-assumption / STS pattern. Bearer token = no audit trail at trust boundary, no per-call identity. Use IAM sigv4 + assumed role with `sts:ExternalId`, not static token.
claim.owner_summary: Cross-account PII egress (billing AWS → marketing AWS) lacks IAM-role-assumption / STS pattern. Bearer token = no audit trail at trust boundary, no per-call identity. Use IAM sigv4 + assumed role with `sts:ExternalId`, not static token.
claim.category: security, authn/authz, API design, data privacy/compliance, rollout/observability, code correctness, testing, schema design

issues.text: Cross-account PII egress (billing AWS → marketing AWS) lacks IAM-role-assumption / STS pattern. Bearer token = no audit trail at trust boundary, no per-call identity. Use IAM sigv4 + assumed role with `sts:ExternalId`, not static token.
issues.owner_summary: Cross-account PII egress (billing AWS → marketing AWS) lacks IAM-role-assumption / STS pattern. Bearer token = no audit trail at trust boundary, no per-call identity. Use IAM sigv4 + assumed role with `sts:ExternalId`, not static token.

claim.summary: The endpoint is designed as an unbounded bulk export. Returning "every active user" as a single list with no pagination, cursoring, or bounded export mechanism will become unreliable as the subscriber base grows, regardless of the low QPS assumption.

claim.summary: The proposed handler is not implementable safely as written. `db.users.find(...)` suggests PyMongo, but the code then uses Django-style `.filter(...)` and `__gte` syntax, while `tier` and `active_since` remain unvalidated strings.

claim.summary: The implementation violates the stated response contract by returning `user.to_dict()` instead of an explicit projection of approved fields. That can leak unintended fields and makes the API unstable for the Lambda consumer.
